description: Analyse drivers of economic growth — production function, growth accounting, capital deepening vs technology, classical/neoclassical/endogenous growth theories, convergence

potential_growth_importance.investor_implication: Equity returns ≈ EPS growth ≈ GDP growth long-term

production_function.diminishing_returns_capital: Marginal product of capital declines as K rises

growth_accounting.tfp_residual: Solow residual = output growth not explained by factor inputs

capital_deepening_vs_tech.capital_deepening: Rising K/L; subject to diminishing returns
capital_deepening_vs_tech.technological_progress: Shifts production function up; only sustainable source of long-run per-capita growth

classical_model.malthusian: Population growth absorbs output gains; living standards stagnate

neoclassical_solow.steady_state: All economies converge to per-capita growth rate equal to TFP growth
neoclassical_solow.convergence: Poorer countries grow faster (β-convergence) given same fundamentals

endogenous_growth.constant_returns_capital_broad: Including human capital and R&D investment
endogenous_growth.no_steady_state_decline: Policy can permanently raise growth via R&D incentives

convergence_types.absolute: All economies converge regardless of starting point
convergence_types.conditional: Convergence only among economies with similar fundamentals

errors.message: growth_model must be classical, neoclassical, or endogenous

anti_patterns.rule: Do not extrapolate developing-economy convergence forecasts uncritically
anti_patterns.why: Convergence is conditional on institutions, savings rate, and human capital; weak fundamentals mean a poor country can stay poor indefinitely

anti_patterns.rule: Do not attribute all residual growth to TFP
anti_patterns.why: Solow residual also captures measurement error in K and L; treat as upper bound on technology contribution

anti_patterns.rule: Do not confuse capital deepening with technological progress
anti_patterns.why: Capital deepening hits diminishing returns; only TFP sustains long-run per-capita growth
